fullData.summary:
*   **Company:** Nscale (London-based AI hyperscaler and large-scale cloud provider specializing in AI data centres).
*   **Funding Amount:** Raised $2bn (£1.5bn) in a Series C funding round.
*   **Valuation:** Reached a $14.6bn valuation less than three years after its first investment.
*   **Founding Date:** Founded in 2023.
*   **CEO/Founder:** Josh Payne.
*   **Lead Investors:** Aker ASA (Norwegian industrial group) and 8090 Industries (New York).
*   **Supporting Investors:** NVIDIA, Dell Technologies, Lenovo, Citadel, Jane Street, Nokia, and Point72.
*   **Strategic Partners:** OpenAI and NVIDIA provided financial and strategic support.
*   **Key Project:** Tapped by OpenAI to build Stargate Norway (the European arm of OpenAI’s global data centre programme).
*   **Capital Usage:** Funds will be used to expand the AI infrastructure platform in Europe, North America, and Asia.
*   **Future Outlook:** CEO Josh Payne predicts AI integration into every industry, product, and job over the next five years.
*   **Related News Dates & Topics (Sidebar):**
    *   UK under-16s social media ban policy discussion: Wed 25 Feb 2026.
    *   Northern scale-up struggle / AI surge: Tue 24 Feb 2026.
    *   Canva acquires Manchester animation software group Cavalry: Tue 24 Feb 2026.
    *   OpenAI names London as its next major research hub: Thu 26 Feb 2026.
*   **Website Actions Available:** Register, Log in, Manage privacy settings (cookies), Accept/Deny consent options.

*   **Funding & Valuation:** London-based AI hyperscaler Nscale has raised $2bn (£1.5bn) in its Series C round, achieving a $14.6bn valuation less than three years after founding.
*   **Investors & Partners:** The round was led by Aker ASA and 8090 Industries with support from NVIDIA, Dell, and Lenovo, while OpenAI tapped Nscale to build the Stargate Norway data centre.
*   **Strategic Expansion:** Capital raised will be used to expand AI infrastructure platforms across Europe, North America, and Asia to support the integration of AI into every industry over the next five years.
